question_text: Explain why high-frequency sound waves have shorter wavelengths than low-frequency sound waves, even though they both travel through air at approximately the same speed.
correct_answer: Since speed = frequency × wavelength, and the speed of sound in air is approximately constant, frequency and wavelength must be inversely proportional. When frequency increases, wavelength must decrease to maintain the same speed. Therefore, high-frequency waves have shorter wavelengths and low-frequency waves have longer wavelengths.
explanation: This requires students to apply the wave equation and understand the inverse relationship between frequency and wavelength when speed is constant. It connects mathematical reasoning to physical properties of sound.
